content.problemStatement:
Chef is currently facing the north direction. Each second he rotates exactly 909090 degrees in clockwise direction. Find the direction in which Chef is facing after exactly XXX seconds.

Note:\textbf{Note}:Note: There are only 4 directions: North, East, South, West (in clockwise order).


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains of a single integer XXX.


Output Format
For each testcase, output the direction in which Chef is facing after exactly XXX seconds.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer X, the number of seconds Chef rotates.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a separate line the direction Chef is facing after X seconds. The possible outputs are: North, East, South, West.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Starting from North, each second rotates 90° clockwise.\n1 second → East, 3 seconds → West, 6 seconds (6 mod 4 = 2) → South.

2→South (2 mod 4), 4→North (0), 7→West (3), 10→North (2 mod 4 = 2? actually 10 mod 4 = 2 → South, correction)
Correction: 10 mod 4 = 2, so output should be South. Updated output: "South
North
West
South".

solution.approach: Each second Chef rotates 909090 degrees clockwise. 909090 mod 360 = 90 degrees, i.e., a quarter turn. Therefore after X seconds Chef has turned X * 90 degrees clockwise. Since four quarter turns bring him back to the original direction, only X mod 4 matters. Map the remainder to the corresponding direction: 0 → North, 1 → East, 2 → South, 3 → West.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
